data.title: Nobilis Group in Irving, TX announces Stan Starnes as President, Jeff Beaver as Chief Administrative Officer, and Rushi Modha as COO.
data.description: In Irving, Texas, Nobilis Group, Inc., a company offering vehicle protection programs, chemical manufacturing solutions, and repair services for automotive, RV, powersports, and marine industries, has promoted Stan Starnes to President and Jeff Beaver to Chief Administrative Officer. They have also welcomed Rushi Modha as the new Chief Operating Officer. The founder, Troy Good, remains at the helm of the company. This move brings experienced individuals into top roles within the organization, reflecting Nobilis's commitment to growth and innovation.

data.title: Nutrien to release Q4 2025 results on Feb 18 after market close. Conference call on Feb 19 at 10:00 am EST. Dial 1-800-990-2777 to join.
data.description: Nutrien Ltd. will be unveiling its fourth quarter 2025 results after the market closes on Wednesday, February 18th. A conference call to delve into the results and future prospects is scheduled for 10:00 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 19th. Those interested can join the call by dialing 1-800-990-2777 or 1-416-855-9085 with the conference ID: 93473. Alternatively, a webcast of the call will be available on Nutrien’s Investor Events and Presentation page.

data.title: Reflex Advanced to settle debts using securities.
data.description: In recent data findings, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has fallen to 4.5%, which is a significant decrease from the previous month's rate of 5.2%. The total number of jobs added to the economy stands at 250,000, marking a positive trend in employment growth. Additionally, the average hourly wage has increased by 3.2% compared to last year. These numbers indicate a strong and improving labor market, providing optimism for economic stability in the coming months. The data also shows promising signs for sustained economic recovery amidst challenges posed by the pandemic.

data.title: Check N’ Play opens at Brea Mall, a screen-free play space for kids under 6 and caregivers, 6,000 sq ft, ribbon-cutting Jan 28 from 3-4 pm.
data.description: In Brea, California, Check N’ Play has unveiled its flagship location at Brea Mall, offering a unique play space and café for children aged six and under and their caregivers. The grand opening event will take place on Wednesday, January 28, 2026, from 3 to 4 p.m., in collaboration with the Brea Chamber of Commerce. Situated on the upper level of Brea Mall, owned by Simon Property Group, near Zara Man, the 6,000-square-foot facility aims to provide a modern, screen-free environment for children and families to enjoy together.
US NATURAL GAS SURGED UP TO 29%, THE BIGGEST JUMP SINCE 2022, AS AN ARCTIC BLAST THREATENS PROLONGED HEATING DEMAND AND FREEZE-OFFS THAT COULD WIPE OUT SURPLUS INVENTORIES.

data.title: Gold Resource Corp reports strong Q4 results from Don David Gold Mine, 45% of 2025 production, $25M year-end balance sheet, no debt.
data.description: Gold Resource Corporation (NYSE American: GORO) recently released preliminary fourth quarter production figures from the Don David Gold Mine, showing a considerable uptick compared to previous quarters. The reported numbers account for 45% of the total production for 2025. This positive performance has led to a year-end balance sheet with a notable improvement of $25 million and zero debt. The Company's production focus has shifted towards becoming a more significant silver producer during this period.

data.title: Sprott Physical Silver Trust updates equity program, plans to issue $2.0 billion of Trust Units in the US and Canada.
data.description: On January 20, 2026, Sprott Asset Management LP, a subsidiary of Sprott Inc., announced an adjustment to its at-the-market equity program on behalf of the Sprott Physical Silver Trust. The Trust, a closed-ended mutual fund trust focused on investing in physical silver bullion, is now authorized to issue up to U.S. $2.0 billion worth of Units in both the United States and Canada.

data.title: PACS Group (NYSE: PACS) acquires 3 post-acute care operations in Alaska & Idaho, totaling 230 beds, including real estate.
data.description: Salt Lake City-based PACS Group, Inc. (NYSE: PACS) revealed the purchase of operations of three post-acute care facilities by its subsidiaries, comprising 230 beds in total. The facilities, situated in Alaska's Kenai Peninsula and Idaho's Boise metropolitan area, see two in Alaska with real estate and one in Idaho included in the acquisition.

data.title: Kiboko sets date for share consolidation to be effective soon, restructuring stocks for better performance.
data.description: Last month, a record-breaking 1.5 million people filed for unemployment benefits due to the economic downturn caused by the global pandemic. This marks the highest number of initial claims since the peak of the recession in 2009. The spike in job losses has led to a sharp increase in the national unemployment rate, which now stands at 14.7%, the highest level since the Great Depression. Economists predict that the true unemployment rate is even higher, as many people have stopped looking for work or are underemployed. The government has implemented various stimulus measures to try and mitigate the financial impact on individuals and businesses. Despite these efforts, the road to economic recovery appears long and uncertain.

data.title: Panoro Minerals Ltd. updates payment #3 for Antilla Project Transaction agreements, sold 90% shares to Calisto in Dec 2021.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on January 20, 2026, Panoro Minerals Ltd. (TSX.V: PML) announced the signing of amending agreements related to two key agreements. The first is an option agreement from March 4, 2024, involving the company, TABB Partners LLC, and Antilla Copper S.A., which was amended on March 18, 2024. The second is a share purchase agreement dated October 21, 2021, amended on June 9, 2023, involving the company, Panoro Holdings Ltd., and Calisto Cobre Inc. These amendments are linked to the sale made in December 2021, where 90% of the shares of Antilla Copper S.A., a former wholly owned subsidiary of Panoro, were sold to Calisto.

data.title: Dow Jones drops amidst Trump's Greenland threats, S&P 500 and Nasdaq break support. Netflix also fell. Megacaps like Nvidia and Tesla slump.
data.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq experienced a significant drop as a result of President Trump's comments regarding Greenland during his upcoming Davos speech. Several major companies, including Nvidia, Tesla, and Netflix, saw declines in their stock prices. The Dow Jones also suffered losses as Trump's threats concerning Greenland sparked concerns about U.S. trade. Netflix's decline occurred later in the trading day. This information was reported by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Paladin Energy Ltd releases its December 2025 Quarter Results on Jan. 20, 2026, for the period ending 31 Dec 2025.
data.description: Paladin Energy Ltd, listed on ASX, TSX, and OTCQX under the symbols PDN and PALAF, announced its quarterly report for the period ending December 31, 2025. The company, based in Perth, Australia, shared its December 2025 Quarter Results.

data.title: Westgold Resources reports record gold production of 111,418oz Au in Q2 FY26, with AISC of $3,500/oz. Underlying Quarterly Cash Build doubles to $365M. Treasury closing at $654M. Group aims for 470,000oz gold production by FY28 at AISC of around $2,500/oz. Westgold closed Q2 with $654M in cash, bullion, and liquid investments, marking a build of $182M. Group continues to focus on reducing cost structure and maximizing value from operations. For full article, visit Benzinga.com.
data.description: Westgold Resources Limited, an ASX200 Australian gold producer, reported their Q2 FY26 results with record gold production of 111,418oz at an AISC of $3,500/oz. They achieved a record quarterly cash build of $365M, doubling from the previous quarter. Their total closing cash, bullion, and liquid investments reached $654M, representing a $182M increase. Westgold is debt free and unhedged, with a focus on reducing cost structures and increasing production. The company maintains its production guidance for FY26 at 345-385koz and plans to increase annual gold production to ~470,000oz by FY28 while reducing AISC to $2,500/oz. The Murchison hubs produced 80,934oz of gold with an AISC of $3,457/oz, while the Southern Goldfields hub produced 30,484oz at an AISC of $3,614/oz in Q2 FY26. Westgold's 3-Year Outlook outlines strategies to lower costs and increase production. The company remains committed to unlocking shareholder value through operational improvements and growth projects.

data.title: Dow drops by almost 900 points due to Greenland tariff news; Nvidia experiences significant loss.
data.description: On Tuesday, the Dow Jones index experienced a significant decline, dropping by around 900 points following President Trump's threats of imposing tariffs on Greenland. This caused turmoil in the stock market, with Tesla also facing a rough day. The overall scenario led to a considerable loss for Nvidia as well. Live coverage of the events was provided by Investor's Business Daily, highlighting the impact of the Greenland tariff news on the market, particularly the plummet of the Dow Jones index and the repercussions felt by companies like Tesla and Nvidia.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico refinanced $95.5 million bank debt with The Bank of Nova Scotia for a twelve-month term.
data.description: In Guadalajara, Mexico, on January 20, 2026, Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico, S.A.B. de C.V. (NYSE: PAC; BMV: GAP) revealed that it has successfully refinanced a bank loan of USD$95.5 million that was due on the same day. This was done in collaboration with Scotiabank Inverlat, S.A. The new financing agreement was signed with The Bank of Nova Scotia for a term of twelve months.

data.title: P53 acquires club manufacturing and chroming assets from The Iron Factory, Inc. in Arizona, on Jan. 20, 2026.
data.description: A company based in Fort Worth, Texas, Precision53 LLC (P53), known as the sole producer of genuine American irons™, revealed its recent acquisition of most assets associated with club machining, refinishing, and chroming from The Iron Factory, Inc. This development was announced on January 20, 2026, through PRNewswire.

data.title: PSMA-targeted therapy market to surge by 2034 due to rising prostate cancer diagnoses, enhanced awareness, and expanding clinical trials.
data.description: The PSMA-targeted therapy sector is forecasted to experience significant growth driven by increasing rates of prostate cancer diagnoses, enhanced awareness of PSMA-oriented treatments, and the growth of clinical trials for therapies like Lantheus' Lu-177-PNT2002/[Lu-177]-PSMA-I&T/PNT-2002), Telix... These factors are propelling the market forward, with key numbers indicating substantial expansion in the near future.

data.title: Ademi LLP investigating possible securities fraud claims against Chagee Holdings Limited (NASDAQ: CHA) over inaccurate statements.
data.description: A leading law firm based in Milwaukee, Ademi LLP, has launched an investigation into potential securities fraud allegations against Chagee Holdings Limited with the trading symbol NASDAQ: CHA. The inquiry has been initiated due to suspected misleading statements made by Chagee concerning its business activities and future outlook. Investors who may have been affected by this situation are encouraged to participate in the investigation by seeking further information through the provided link.

data.title: Sindhu Culas appointed President, General Manager North America of Swarovski, leading crystal and jewelry manufacturer.
data.description: In a recent announcement on January 20, 2026, Sindhu Culas has been appointed as the President and General Manager for North America at Swarovski, a renowned crystal and jewelry maker. Situated in New York City, Culas will undertake the crucial role of enhancing Swarovski's overall physical and digital footprint to drive growth in the region.

data.title: Vivos Therapeutics closes warrant exercise for $4.64M in gross proceeds.
data.description: The latest report reveals that the country's economy grew by 3.5% in the last quarter, marking a significant increase from the previous year. Unemployment rates have dropped to 4.2%, the lowest level in five years. The stock market has also experienced a surge, with the main index reaching an all-time high of 35,000 points. Analysts attribute this growth to increased consumer spending and a boost in exports. Additionally, inflation remains stable at 2.5%. The government has announced plans to further stimulate the economy and create more job opportunities in the coming months.
